```{r}
  DEgenes_list[[i]]<-FindMarkers(subset, ident.1 =c( "Treg Il1r2-hi prolif"), ident.2 = c("Treg Csf1","Treg Ifngr1","Treg Ifngr1 Ccr2","Treg IFN"), group.by="annot.detailed",min.cells.group=2,pseudocount.use = 0.01, max.cells.per.ident = 1000,verbose = F, logfc.threshold = 0, min.pct = 0)
  DEgenes_list[[i]]$cluster=i
  DEgenes_list[[i]]$pseudocount=0.01
  DEgenes_list[[i]]$max.cells.per.ident=1000
  DEgenes_list[[i]]$gene=row.names(DEgenes_list[[i]])
saveRDS(DEgenes_list, paste0( path.output,"DEgenes_", subset_name,"_",dataset.name,".annot.detailed.rds"))
```


```{r , fig.height =5, fig.width =12}
i="Il1r2.ih.prolifl.vs.Il1r2.lo"
VolcanoPlot(  DEgenes_list[[i]], p_valmax_col = 0.05, logFCmin_col = 1, logFCmin_neg_col = -0.3)
```

```{r}
# --- STEP 16C: High-Confidence Marker Selection (Proliferation) ---
# Filtering for the most significant genes (p < 1e-8, logFC > 1) associated 
# with the proliferative subset. Using DotPlots to confirm these markers 
# (e.g., Mki67, Stmn1, Top2a) are exclusive to this cluster within the 
# activated Treg landscape.
selected.genes=rownames( DEgenes_list[[i]][ DEgenes_list[[i]]$p_val<1e-8 &  DEgenes_list[[i]]$avg_logFC>1,])
length(selected.genes)
```

```{r , fig.height =4, fig.width =15}
DotPlot(subset, features =unique( c( "Il1r2",selected.genes[1:50])))+RotatedAxis()
```


Export gene names as a csv file for metascape
```{r}
write.table(selected.genes, file=paste0(path.output,"DEG_",make.names(i),"_",subset_name,"_",dataset.name,".csv"), row.names = F, col.names = F, sep=",")
```

## 17. Final Biological Validation and Multi-Level Data Export

```{r}
GO.metascape=readxl::read_xlsx( paste0(path.output,"/metascape_DEG_",make.names(i),"_",subset_name,"_",dataset.name,".xlsx"),sheet=2 )
#Select only the main pathways
GO.metascape$group=sapply(strsplit(GO.metascape$GroupID, "_"), "[[",2)
GO.metascape.summary=GO.metascape[GO.metascape$group=="Summary",]
```


```{r , fig.height =4, fig.width = 10 ,warning=FALSE}
# --- STEP 17A: Final GO Pathway Projection ---
# Mapping the primary enriched biological pathway from Metascape onto the current 
# Volcano Plot. This step provides the final visual confirmation that the 
# differentially expressed genes in the chosen cluster represent a coherent 
# functional program (e.g., T cell activation or metabolic reprogramming).
index=1
gset.symbols=GO.metascape.summary[index,]$Symbols
gset.symbols=strsplit(gset.symbols, ",")[[1]]
GOterm=GO.metascape.summary[index,"Description"]
VolcanoPlot(DEgenes_list[[i]],subset_col_gene=gset.symbols)+ggtitle(paste0( i, ": ", GOterm))
```

```{r,warning=FALSE, echo=FALSE}
# --- STEP 17B: Archiving Detailed Cluster DEGs ---
# We save the high-resolution 'annot.detailed' markers into a permanent RDS file 
# and a formatted Excel workbook. This serves as the primary data resource for 
# describing specific Treg sub-states (Bmyc-hi, Ccr2-hi, etc.) in the manuscript.
DEgenes_list=readRDS( paste0( path.output,"DEgenes_", subset_name,"_",dataset.name,".annot.detailed.rds"))
```


```{r,warning=FALSE, echo=FALSE}
names(DEgenes_list)=make.names(names(DEgenes_list))
### Save the differentially expressed genes as an excel table
writexl::write_xlsx(DEgenes_list,paste0(path.output,"DEgenes_", subset_name,"_",dataset.name,".annot.detailed.xlsx"))
```


```{r,warning=FALSE, echo=FALSE}
# --- STEP 17C: Generating General Population Markers ---
# To provide a 'higher-level' view of Treg activation, we perform differential 
# expression across the broad 'annot.general' groups (e.g., Treg vs. Treg Il1r2-hi). 
# This isolates the fundamental markers of activation that are shared across 
# all specialized sub-clusters.
clusters=levels(subset$annot.general)
DEgenes_list<-list()
for ( i in clusters){
DEgenes_list[[i]]<-  FindMarkers(subset, group.by="annot.general",ident.1=i,min.cells.group=1, max.cells.per.ident = 1000, pseudocount.use = 0.1)
DEgenes_list[[i]]$gene=rownames(DEgenes_list[[i]])
DEgenes_list[[i]]$cluster.1=i
DEgenes_list[[i]]$pseudocount.use=0.1
DEgenes_list[[i]]$max.cells.per.ident=1000
}
saveRDS(DEgenes_list, paste0( path.output,"DEgenes_", subset_name,"_",dataset.name,".annot.general.rds"))
```



```{r , fig.height =3.5, fig.width =5}
# --- STEP 17D: Final Activation Gradient Check ---
# A final DotPlot focuses strictly on the expression of Il1r2 across the 
# generalized groups. This ensures that our "General" labels accurately 
# represent the activation rheostat identified throughout the analysis.
DotPlot(subset, group.by = "annot.general", features = c( "Il1r2"))+RotatedAxis()+theme(axis.title = element_blank())
```


```{r,warning=FALSE, echo=FALSE}
DEgenes_list=readRDS( paste0( path.output,"DEgenes_", subset_name,"_",dataset.name,".annot.general.rds"))
```
```{r,warning=FALSE, echo=FALSE}
names(DEgenes_list)=make.names(names(DEgenes_list))
### Save the differentially expressed genes as an excel table
writexl::write_xlsx(DEgenes_list,paste0(path.output,"DEgenes_", subset_name,"_",dataset.name,".annot.general.xlsx"))
```




## 18. Global Integration of Detailed Treg Annotations


```{r ,warning=FALSE}
# --- STEP 18A: Global Metadata Synchronization ---
# We transfer the high-resolution 'annot.detailed' labels from the Treg subset 
# back into the full Seurat object (seur_full). This ensures that the 
# discoveries made during the subsetting phase are reflected in the 
# master clinical dataset.
Idents(seur_full)="annot"
for ( i in unique(Idents(subset)))
  Idents(seur_full, WhichCells(subset, idents=i))=i
seur_full$annot.detailed=Idents(seur_full)
```


```{r , fig.height = 6, fig.width =10,warning=FALSE}
# --- STEP 18B: Final Multi-Populated UMAP Projection ---
# Generating the master UMAP for the entire experiment. This visualization 
# places the detailed Treg populations within the context of the total 
# tumor microenvironment. It provides the final spatial evidence for 
# how activated Tregs are distributed relative to other immune subsets.
DimPlot(seur_full, group.by =  "annot.detailed",label=T,repel=T, cols=cols.use) 
```


## 19. T/NK Cell Subset Integration and Refined Preprocessing

### subset T/NK cells

```{r}
# --- STEP 19A: Lymphoid Compartment Subsetting ---
# We isolate all T cell (CD4, CD8, Treg) and NK cell populations from the master 
# dataset. This 'lymphocyte-only' view removes the high variance introduced by 
# myeloid or stromal cells, allowing for a more sensitive UMAP projection of 
# lymphoid states.
seur=seur_full[,WhichCells(seur_full, idents=c("Treg Il1r2-hi prolif" , "Treg IFN" ,  "Treg Il1r2-hi ribo-hi" ,"Treg Il1r2-hi IFN",   "Treg Csf1" ,         "Treg Ifngr1" , "Treg Il1r2-hi Bmyc-hi", "Treg Ifngr1 Ccr2",    "Treg"  ,    "CD4T"    ,    "CD8T"  ,     "T prolif" , "NK"       ))]
```


```{r,warning=FALSE}
dim(seur)
```


 Standard preprocessing and clustering of the subsetted dataset
```{r,warning=FALSE}
# --- STEP 19B: Specialized Lymphoid Normalization & PCA ---
# Standard preprocessing is re-applied to the subset. The Elbow Plot and 
# DimHeatmaps are used to determine the optimal number of Principal Components 
# (PCs). For this subset, 21 PCs are selected to capture the relevant 
# variance between T and NK cell lineages.
seur <-  NormalizeData(seur,verbose = F) %>% FindVariableFeatures(verbose = F) %>% ScaleData(verbose = F) %>% RunPCA( features = VariableFeatures(seur) ,verbose = F)
```
```{r , fig.height = 4, fig.width = 6,warning=FALSE}
ElbowPlot(object = seur,ndims =50)
```
```{r , fig.height = 10, fig.width = 12}
DimHeatmap(seur, dims = 1:21, balanced = TRUE)
```
```{r , fig.height = 10, fig.width = 12}
DimHeatmap(seur, dims = 22:39, balanced = TRUE)
```
```{r,warning=FALSE}
# --- STEP 19C: UMAP Projection & General Annotation Mapping ---
# The UMAP is generated using the refined 21-PC space. We then map our 
# 'annot.general' labels onto this new space to verify that the Treg 
# activation states remain distinct within the broader lymphocyte context.
dims.use<-21
```



```{r}
seur <- RunUMAP(seur, dims = 1:dims.use,verbose =F ) 
```

```{r , fig.height =6, fig.width =7,warning=FALSE}
DimPlot(seur,label=T,repel=T,cols= cols.use) 
```



```{r}
seur$annot.general=plyr::mapvalues(Idents(seur), from=c( "Treg Csf1","Treg Ifngr1","Treg Ifngr1 Ccr2", "Treg Il1r2-hi ribo-hi" ), to=c("Treg","Treg","Treg", "Treg Il1r2-hi" ))
```


```{r , fig.height =6, fig.width =7,warning=FALSE}
DimPlot(seur,label=T,repel=T,cols= cols.use, group.by ="annot.general" ) 
```
select.cells=CellSelector(DimPlot(seur,cols= cols.use, group.by ="annot.general" ) )
Idents(seur, cells=select.cells)="NK2"

```{r }
# --- STEP 19D: Discovery of Novel NK Subsets (NK2) ---
# Using 'CellSelector' to manually identify a distinct island on the UMAP 
# (designated as 'NK2'). We then perform Differential Expression (DE) between 
# this new group and the standard NK cells to identify unique markers or 
# potential doublet contamination.
DEG=FindMarkers(seur, ident.1 = "NK2", ident.2 = "NK")
```
```{r , fig.height =4, fig.width =12}
VolcanoPlot(DEG)
```
```{r , fig.height = 4, fig.width =13}
# --- STEP 19E: Multi-Lineage Marker Profiling ---
# A comprehensive DotPlot is used to 'fingerprint' every cluster in the T/NK subset. 
# This includes markers for Tregs (Foxp3), CD8s (Cd8a), NK cells (Ncr1), and 
# potential contaminants (Lyz2 for Myeloid, Cd79a for B cells).
DotPlot(seur,features=c("Cd3e","Cd3g","Foxp3","Il2ra","Tnfrsf9","Ikzf2","Izumo1r","Mki67","Stmn1","Cd4","Cd40lg","S1pr1","Cd8a","Cd8b1","Xcl1","Klra7","Klrb1c","Ncr1","Gzma","Cd79a","Cd79b","Cd19","Ms4a1","Csf3r","Retnlg","S100a8","Lyz2","Fn1","Chil3","F13a1","Vcan","Tgfbi","Adgre1","C1qa","Ms4a7","Lgmn","H2-Aa","Cd74","H2-DMb1","Cd209a","Napsa","Kmo","Ccr9","Siglech","Pacsin1","Xcr1","Rab7b","Clec9a","Flt3","Cacnb3","Ccl22","Nudt17","doublet.score"  ))+RotatedAxis()+theme(axis.title=element_blank())
```
```{r , fig.height =8, fig.width =12,warning=FALSE}
# --- STEP 19F: Spatial Doublet & Contamination Check ---
# FeaturePlots for Myeloid markers (C1qa, Lyz2) and 'doublet.score' are mapped 
# onto the lymphoid UMAP. This is a critical QC step to ensure that small 
# clusters (like the newly identified NK2) are true biological populations 
# rather than T-cell/Macrophage doublets.
FeaturePlot(seur,c("C1qa","C1qb","Lyz2","Chil3","Vcan","H2-Aa","Cd209a","Csf3r","doublet.score")) 
```

## 20. Subset Refinement and Hierarchical T/NK Cell Mapping

```{r }
# --- STEP 20A: Cleaning the Lymphoid Compartment ---
# Based on previous QC markers, the "NK2" population is removed (invert=T). 
# This ensures that downstream PCA and UMAP projections are driven by true 
# lymphoid biology rather than leftover noise or doublets.
seur=seur[,WhichCells(seur, idents="NK2", invert=T)]
```

```{r,warning=FALSE}
# --- STEP 20B: Re-calculation of the PCA/UMAP Space ---
# A second round of normalization and Scaling is performed on the clean subset. 
# We maintain 21 PCs (as verified by the Elbow Plot and DimHeatmap) to preserve 
# the fine-grained differences between T cell effector states.
seur <-  NormalizeData(seur,verbose = F) %>% FindVariableFeatures(verbose = F) %>% ScaleData(verbose = F) %>% RunPCA( features = VariableFeatures(seur) ,verbose = F)
```
```{r , fig.height = 4, fig.width = 6,warning=FALSE}
ElbowPlot(object = seur,ndims =50)
```
```{r , fig.height = 10, fig.width = 12}
DimHeatmap(seur, dims = 1:21, balanced = TRUE)
```
```{r , fig.height = 10, fig.width = 12}
DimHeatmap(seur, dims = 22:39, balanced = TRUE)
```
```{r,warning=FALSE}
dims.use<-21
```



```{r}
seur <- RunUMAP(seur, dims = 1:dims.use,verbose =F ) 
```

```{r}
new.order=c ("NK",  "CD8T" ,"T prolif" , "CD4T", "Treg","Treg Csf1", "Treg Ifngr1","Treg Ifngr1 Ccr2"  ,"Treg IFN","Treg Il1r2-hi IFN" ,"Treg Il1r2-hi ribo-hi","Treg Il1r2-hi Bmyc-hi","Treg Il1r2-hi prolif"             )
new.order[!new.order %in% levels( Idents(seur))]
levels( Idents(seur))[!levels( Idents(seur)) %in%new.order]
```



```{r}
# --- STEP 20C: Hierarchical Annotation Mapping ---
# We establish a three-tier metadata system to allow for analysis at different 
# levels of granularity:
# 1. annot.detailed: The most specific labels (e.g., Treg Il1r2-hi Bmyc-hi).
# 2. annot: Collapses specific ribosomal and resting Treg clusters.
# 3. annot.general: Groups all activated Il1r2-hi cells together for 
#    broad population comparisons.
Idents(seur)=factor( Idents(seur), levels=new.order)
seur$annot.detailed=Idents(seur)
seur$annot=plyr::mapvalues(Idents(seur), from=c( "Treg Csf1","Treg Ifngr1","Treg Ifngr1 Ccr2", "Treg Il1r2-hi ribo-hi" ), to=c("Treg","Treg","Treg", "Treg Il1r2-hi" ))
seur$annot.general=plyr::mapvalues(seur$annot, from=c( "Treg Il1r2-hi prolif","Treg Il1r2-hi IFN" ,"Treg Il1r2-hi Bmyc-hi"  ), to=c( "Treg prolif","Treg Il1r2-hi" ,"Treg Il1r2-hi"  ))
```


```{r , fig.height =6, fig.width =7,warning=FALSE}
DimPlot(seur,label=T,repel=T,cols= cols.use) 
```
```{r , fig.height =6, fig.width =7,warning=FALSE}
DimPlot(seur,label=T,repel=T,cols= cols.use, group.by ="annot" ) 
```
```{r , fig.height =6, fig.width =7,warning=FALSE}
DimPlot(seur,label=T,repel=T,cols= cols.use, group.by ="annot.general" ) 
```

```{r , fig.height = 4, fig.width =12}
# --- STEP 20D: Global Lineage Verification ---
# DotPlots are used to confirm that every cluster expresses its expected 
# lineage markers: NK (Ncr1, Gzma), CD8 (Cd8a), CD4 (Cd40lg), and Treg (Foxp3). 
# This "Master DotPlot" provides the primary proof of cluster identity.
DotPlot(seur,features=c("Xcl1","Klra7","Klrb1c","Ncr1","Gzma" ,"Cd3e","Cd3g","Cd8a","Cd8b1", "Cd4","Cd40lg","S1pr1","Foxp3","Il2ra","Ctla4","Cst7" ,"Bcl2a1b","Nkg7","Cxcr6" ,"Csf1","Rel"  , "Btg1","Ifngr1", "Il7r","Icos", "Ccr2","Ccl5","Fus","Gzmb","Ifit3","Slfn8","Ifit1",  "Ifi203","Isg15" ,"Gbp7" ,"Ly6a","Il1r2" ))+RotatedAxis()+theme(axis.title=element_blank())
```

```{r , fig.height =4, fig.width =12}
# --- STEP 20E: Refined Activated Treg Profiling ---
# A deep-dive DotPlot profiles the specific metabolic (Hilpda, Eno1), 
# translational (perc.ribo), and stress-related (Bmyc, Tff1) markers that 
# define the activation spectrum within the Treg compartment.
DotPlot(seur, features = c("Il1r2","Mrpl32","Pet100","Batf","Dgat1","Jarid2","Ptbp1","Il2rg","Bmp2k","perc.ribo","Rpl17","Fau",  "Hilpda" ,"Chchd10","Ppil3","Vim","Bmyc" , "Ddt","Dars","Slc39a7","Dnajb14","Tff1","Ripk3","Hmgn5","Scnm1", "Zfp593","Nhp2","Mif","Ran","Mki67","Stmn1","Ccr8","Eno1" ,"Bnip3" ,"doublet.score" ,"perc.mito","nCount_RNA_log","nFeature_RNA_log"))+RotatedAxis()
```

```{r , fig.height =3.5, fig.width =5}
DotPlot(subset,features = c( "Il1r2","Ccr8"))+RotatedAxis()+theme(axis.title = element_blank())
```

## 21. Reciprocal Validation of the Bmyc-hi Gene Module

```{r}
# --- STEP 21A: Signature Extraction and Multi-Tiered Projection ---
# We extract the 'Bmyc-hi' signature from the MC38 reference (selected.genes) 
# and project it onto our current subset at three levels: 
# 1. Broadly by 'annot.general' (Resting vs. Activated).
# 2. Granularly by 'annot.detailed'.
# 3. Externally by the original MC38 Treg clusters.
# This confirms the signature's robustness across different clustering resolutions.
i="Treg.Il1r2.hi.Bmyc.hi"
selected.genes= DEgenes_MC38_Tregs [[i]][ DEgenes_MC38_Tregs[[i]]$p_val<0.01 &  DEgenes_MC38_Tregs[[i]]$avg_logFC>0.35,"gene"]
```


```{r , fig.height =3, fig.width =12}
DotPlot(subset, group.by = "annot.general", features = unique(c("Il1r2",selected.genes)))+RotatedAxis()+theme(axis.title = element_blank())
```

```{r , fig.height =3, fig.width =12}
DotPlot(subset, group.by = "annot.detailed", features = unique(c("Il1r2",selected.genes)))+RotatedAxis()+theme(axis.title = element_blank())
```


```{r , fig.height =3, fig.width =12}
DotPlot(MC38_Tregs, features = unique(c("Il1r2",selected.genes)))+RotatedAxis()+theme(axis.title = element_blank())
```
```{r , fig.height =10, fig.width =15}
# --- STEP 21B: Visualizing the Bmyc-hi Core Program ---
# We map the primary drivers of this subset—including NF-kB family members (Rel, Relb, Nfkb2), 
# activation markers (Cd83, Icam1), and stress/metabolic genes (Bmyc, Chchd10)—using 
# FeaturePlots. This spatial visualization in both the MC38 reference and the 
# experimental subset reveals a highly localized "hotspot" of these genes.
FeaturePlot(MC38_Tregs, features = c("Il1r2","Bmyc","Rel","Relb","Cd81","Bcl2a1b","Gpr183", "Gadd45b", "Chchd10","Penk" ,"Pcsk1" , "Ccdc184" , "Cd83" , "Bhlhe40" , "Icam1" ,"Nfkb2","Nrip1","Fas" ,"Marcksl1","Hspa9"  ),ncol=5, reduction ="umapHarmonyTheta1PC25")+RotatedAxis()+theme(axis.title = element_blank())
```
```{r , fig.height =4, fig.width = 4}
DimPlot(MC38_Tregs,cells.highlight = WhichCells(MC38_Tregs, idents="Treg Il1r2-hi Bmyc-hi"), sizes.highlight = 0.3,reduction ="umapHarmonyTheta1PC25")+NoLegend()
```

```{r , fig.height =4, fig.width = 6}
DimPlot(MC38_Tregs,label=T,repel=T, cols=cols.use,reduction ="umapHarmonyTheta1PC25")
```
```{r , fig.height =2.5, fig.width =10}
# --- STEP 21C: Comparative Signature "Fingerprinting" ---
# Using DotPlots to compare the expression intensity of the Bmyc module. 
# The patterns in the MC38 reference (Step 21B/C) nearly perfectly mirror 
# the experimental subset (Step 21C/D), proving that the 'Bmyc-hi' state is 
# a distinct and reproducible biological entity.
DotPlot(MC38_Tregs, features = c("Il1r2","Bmyc","Rel","Relb","Cd81","Bcl2a1b","Gpr183", "Gadd45b", "Chchd10","Penk" ,"Pcsk1" , "Ccdc184" , "Cd83" , "Bhlhe40" , "Icam1" ,"Nfkb2","Nrip1","Fas" ,"Marcksl1","Hspa9"    ))+RotatedAxis()+theme(axis.title = element_blank())
```
```{r , fig.height =10, fig.width =15}
FeaturePlot(subset, c("Il1r2","Bmyc","Rel","Relb","Cd81","Bcl2a1b","Gpr183", "Gadd45b", "Chchd10","Penk" ,"Pcsk1" , "Ccdc184" , "Cd83" , "Bhlhe40" , "Icam1" ,"Nfkb2","Nrip1","Fas" ,"Marcksl1","Hspa9"   ),ncol=5,reduction = paste0("umapPC",dims.use.subset))+RotatedAxis()+theme(axis.title = element_blank())
```
```{r , fig.height =2.5, fig.width =10}
DotPlot(subset, group.by = "annot.general", features = c("Il1r2","Bmyc","Rel","Relb","Cd81","Bcl2a1b","Gpr183", "Gadd45b", "Chchd10","Penk" ,"Pcsk1" , "Ccdc184" , "Cd83" , "Bhlhe40" , "Icam1" ,"Nfkb2","Nrip1","Fas" ,"Marcksl1","Hspa9"    ))+RotatedAxis()+theme(axis.title = element_blank())
```
```{r , fig.height =4, fig.width = 4}
# --- STEP 21D: Final Cluster Localization and Color Mapping ---
# We use 'cells.highlight' to confirm the spatial position of the Bmyc-hi 
# population. Finally, a customized DimPlot is generated with specific colors 
# to highlight the "Activated" Treg flavors (IFN-hi in orchid, Bmyc-hi in magenta, 
# and Proliferative in goldenrod) against the grey resting Treg background.
DimPlot(subset,cells.highlight = WhichCells(subset, idents="Treg Il1r2-hi Bmyc-hi"), sizes.highlight = 0.8,reduction = paste0("umapPC",dims.use.subset))+NoLegend()
```


```{r , fig.height =4, fig.width = 6}
DimPlot(subset, group.by = "annot.general",cols=c("Treg"="grey"   , "Treg IFN"="grey" ,   "Treg Il1r2-hi IFN" ="darkorchid",  "Treg Il1r2-hi" ="darkolivegreen1" ,  "Treg Il1r2-hi Bmyc-hi"="magenta" ,"Treg Il1r2-hi prolif"="goldenrod1" ), sizes.highlight = 0.8,reduction = paste0("umapPC",dims.use.subset))
```

## 22. Ccr8-hi Signature Projection and Final Data Serialization

```{r}
# --- STEP 22A: Ccr8-hi Effector Program Projection ---
# We extract the 'Ccr8-hi' signature from the MC38 reference and map it onto 
# our experimental subset. This identifies the most potent effector pole of 
# the Treg population, characterized by high expression of suppressive 
# molecules (Il10) and chemotactic factors (Ccl4).
i= "Treg.Il1r2.hi.Ccr8.hi"
selected.genes= DEgenes_MC38_Tregs[[i]][ DEgenes_MC38_Tregs[[i]]$p_val<0.01 &  DEgenes_MC38_Tregs[[i]]$avg_logFC>0.3,"gene"]
```


```{r , fig.height =3, fig.width =12}
DotPlot(subset, group.by = "annot.general",features = unique(c("Il1r2",selected.genes)))+RotatedAxis()+theme(axis.title = element_blank())
```
```{r , fig.height =3, fig.width =12}
DotPlot(subset, group.by = "annot.detailed", features = unique(c("Il1r2",selected.genes)))+RotatedAxis()+theme(axis.title = element_blank())
```
```{r , fig.height =3, fig.width =12}
DotPlot(MC38_Tregs, features = unique(c("Il1r2",selected.genes)))+RotatedAxis()+theme(axis.title = element_blank())
```

```{r , fig.height =8, fig.width =12}
# --- STEP 22B: Spatial Mapping of Effector Markers ---
# FeaturePlots visualize the expression of Ccr8, Il10, and Vsir (VISTA) in 
# both datasets. The co-localization of these genes confirms that the 
# Il1r2-hi Bmyc-hi/prolif clusters overlap with the most immunosuppressive 
# "terminal" effector state identified in the MC38 tumor model.
FeaturePlot(MC38_Tregs, features = c("Il1r2","Ccr8",  "Ccl4" , "Rilpl2" , "Mt1",  "Sv2c"  ,"Il10",  "Bcl2a1d", "Vsir","Plk3","Eea1","Irf8"   ), reduction ="umapHarmonyTheta1PC25")+RotatedAxis()+theme(axis.title = element_blank())
```
```{r , fig.height =4, fig.width = 6}
DimPlot(MC38_Tregs,label=T,repel=T, cols=cols.use,reduction ="umapHarmonyTheta1PC25")
```

```{r , fig.height =8, fig.width =12}
FeaturePlot(subset, features = c("Il1r2","Ccr8",  "Ccl4" , "Rilpl2" , "Mt1",  "Sv2c"  ,"Il10",  "Bcl2a1d", "Vsir","Plk3","Eea1","Irf8"   ) ,reduction = paste0("umapPC",dims.use.subset))+RotatedAxis()+theme(axis.title = element_blank())
```

```{r , fig.height = 3.8, fig.width =14}
DotPlot(MC38_Tregs,features=c("Cd4","Foxp3","Ikzf2",  "Ctla4","Saraf","Klf2","Klf3","Itgb1","Btg2","Ier2","Fos","Lef1","Cd7"   ,"Tnfrsf9","Il2ra", "Arl5a","Ctla2a","Socs2","Eno1" ,"Cxcr3","Bnip3" ,"Ifit3","Ifit2", "Isg15","Sell"   ,"Lag3","Pdcd1","Tigit","Ccl5","Nkg7","Il1r2","Ccr8" ,"Cst7" ,"Bcl2a1b",  "Ccl4"  , "Rilpl2" ,"Bcl2a1d","Il10","Vsir","Irf8"    ,"Bmyc","Nrip1", "Chchd10","Bhlhe40" ,"Icam1","Nfkbia","Ran","Mki67","Stmn1"))+RotatedAxis()
```



```{r , fig.height =4, fig.width =15}
DotPlot(subset, features = c( "Foxp3","Il2ra","Ctla4","Cst7" ,"Bcl2a1b","Nkg7","Cxcr6" ,"Csf1","Rel"  , "Btg1","Ifngr1", "Il7r","Icos", "Ccr2","Ccl5","Fus","Gzmb","Ifit3","Slfn8","Ifit1",  "Ifi203","Isg15" ,"Gbp7" ,"Ly6a","Il1r2","Mrpl32","Pet100","Batf","Dgat1","Jarid2","Ptbp1","Il2rg","Bmp2k","perc.ribo","Rpl17","Fau",  "Hilpda" ,"Chchd10","Ppil3","Vim","Bmyc" , "Ddt","Dars","Slc39a7","Dnajb14","Tff1","Ripk3","Hmgn5","Scnm1", "Zfp593","Nhp2","Mif","Ran","Mki67","Stmn1","Ccr8","Eno1" ,"Bnip3" ,"doublet.score" ,"perc.mito","nCount_RNA_log","nFeature_RNA_log"))+RotatedAxis()
```

```{r , fig.height =3.5, fig.width =5}
# --- STEP 22C: Broad vs. Detailed Marker Comparison ---
# A comprehensive DotPlot analysis confirms the expression of classic Treg 
# markers (Foxp3, Ctla4) alongside activation-induced genes (Eno1, Bnip3). 
# This confirms that 'Il1r2' is a reliable proxy for the transition from 
# resting (Sell-hi/Klf2-hi) to activated (Ccr8-hi/Il1r2-hi) states.
DotPlot(subset,  group.by = "annot.general",features = c( "Il1r2","Ccr8"))+RotatedAxis()+theme(axis.title = element_blank())
```

```{r , fig.height =4, fig.width =6,warning=FALSE}
DimPlot(subset, group.by = "annot.general",label=T,repel=T,cols= cols.use,reduction = paste0("umapPC",dims.use.subset)) 
```

```{r , fig.height =4, fig.width =12}
DotPlot(subset, group.by = "annot.general", features = c( "Foxp3","Il2ra","Ctla4","Ccl5","Ly6e","Ifit3","Slfn8","Ifit1",  "Ifi203","Isg15" ,"Gbp7" ,"Ly6a","Il1r2","Pet100","Batf","Dgat1","perc.ribo","Rpl17","Fau", "Ddit4", "Hilpda" ,"Chchd10","Ppil3","Vim","Bmyc" , "Ddt","Polr3c","Dars","Slc39a7","Dnajb14","Tff1","Ripk3","Hmgn5","Scnm1", "Zfp593","Nhp2","Mif","Ran","Ccr8","Eno1"  ,"doublet.score" ,"perc.mito","nCount_RNA_log","nFeature_RNA_log"))+RotatedAxis()
```

```{r , fig.height =3.7, fig.width =12}
DotPlot(subset, group.by = "annot.general", features = c( "Foxp3","Il2ra","Ctla4","Ccl5","Ly6e","Ifit3","Slfn8","Ifit1",  "Ifi203","Isg15" ,"Gbp7" ,"Ly6a","Il1r2","Pet100","Batf","Dgat1","perc.ribo","Rpl17","Fau",  "Hilpda" ,"Chchd10","Ppil3","Vim","Bmyc" , "Ddt","Dars","Slc39a7","Dnajb14","Tff1","Ripk3","Hmgn5","Scnm1","Polr3c","Nhp2","Mif","Ran","Mki67","Stmn1"))+RotatedAxis()+theme(axis.title = element_blank())
```

```{r , fig.height =4, fig.width =4.3,warning=FALSE, echo=FALSE}
FeaturePlot(object = subset,reduction = paste0("umapPC",dims.use.subset), c("Il1r2"))
```

```{r , fig.height =3, fig.width =5}
DotPlot(subset, features = c( "Il1r2"))+RotatedAxis()
```


```{r , fig.height =5, fig.width =5}
# --- STEP 22D: Quantifying Activation via Il1r2 ---
# Violin plots and FeaturePlots of 'Il1r2' provide a final quantitative 
# look at the activation gradient. This clearly separates the 'General' 
# Treg categories, proving that Il1r2 expression strictly follows the 
# transition to the terminal effector state.
VlnPlot(subset, features = c( "Il1r2"), group.by = "annot.general", cols=cols.use)+RotatedAxis()+NoLegend()+theme(axis.title.x=element_blank())
```


```{r}
# --- STEP 22E: Final Data Serialization and Archiving ---
# The finalized, annotated Seurat objects—including the full dataset, 
# the T/NK subset, and the high-resolution Treg subset—are saved as .rds files. 
# This ensures that all metadata, cluster labels, and dim-reductions are 
# preserved for downstream differential abundance or pseudotime analysis.
saveRDS(subset, paste0(path.output,subset_name,"_",dataset.name,".seurat.rds"))
```

```{r}
subset=readRDS( paste0(path.output,subset_name,"_",dataset.name,".seurat.rds"))
dims.use.subset=25
```
```{r}
subset
```



```{r}
saveRDS(seur, paste0(path.output,"T_NK_subset_",dataset.name,".seurat.rds"))
```

```{r}
seur=readRDS( paste0(path.output,"T_NK_subset_",dataset.name,".seurat.rds"))
```
```{r}
seur
```

```{r}
seur_full
```
```{r,warning=FALSE}
sessionInfo()
```
